prompt_text:
### Demographics: 61-year-old Asian man

### Visit type: Follow-up visit (12-month follow-up)

### Reason for visit / current symptoms
Reason for visit: Routine follow-up (every 3 months) | Diagnosis context: 8 years ago Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes after preoperative labs showed elevated HbA1c
Current symptoms: Rare nocturnal hypoglycemia (1 episode in 3 months); no DKA/HHS signs; Nausea, reduced appetite, and fatigue over the last 1-2 months with ongoing renal function decline.

### History
- Other conditions: Chronic kidney disease Stage 5 (0-15) (Dialysis: Hemodialysis)
Heart failure: HFimpEF (NYHA Class II)
ASCVD: Peripheral artery disease
Body mass index category: Normal
Diabetic neuropathy
Diabetic Peripheral Neuropathy
Diabetic Foot Ulcer / High-Risk Foot
Recurrent Complicated Urinary Tract Infections
Frailty with Mobility Impairment
Major Depressive Disorder
Cataract (status post surgery)
- Diabetes-related comorbidities: CKD: Stage 5 (0-15) (Dialysis: Hemodialysis); HF: HFimpEF (NYHA Class II); ASCVD: Peripheral artery disease; MASLD: Normal; BMI Category: Normal
- Family history: Type 2 diabetes (older brother)
- Social history: Former smoker (quit 10 years ago; 5 cigarettes/day for 25 years; ~6.2 pack-years); Alcohol 1-2 drinks/month

### Medication information
- Current medications: Semaglutide 1mg once weekly; Glimepiride 2mg once daily; Metformin 1000mg twice daily; Duloxetine (60 mg daily), Rosuvastatin (10 mg daily), Lisinopril (10 mg daily), Ciprofloxacin (500 mg twice daily for 7 days)

### Physical exam (current)
Weight 61.2 kg; Height 171.5 cm; BMI 20.8 kg/m²; Blood pressure 124/88 mmHg

### Laboratory values (with date / relative timing)
Format: YYYY-MM-DD value or relative timing (12 months ago / 9 months ago / 6 months ago / 3 months ago / current)
Order: 12 months ago -> 9 months ago -> 6 months ago -> 3 months ago -> current
- Glycemia: HbA1c (12 months ago 10.2%; 9 months ago 10.5%; 6 months ago 10.0%; 3 months ago 9.8%; current 9.9%), FPG (12 months ago 221mg/dL; 9 months ago 253mg/dL; 6 months ago 286mg/dL; 3 months ago 279mg/dL; current 214mg/dL)
- Anthropometrics / blood pressure: Weight (12 months ago 61.2kg; 9 months ago 58.9kg; 6 months ago 62.1kg; 3 months ago 59.1kg; current 61.2kg), Height (12 months ago 171.5cm; 9 months ago 171.5cm; 6 months ago 171.5cm; 3 months ago 171.5cm; current 171.5cm), BMI (12 months ago 21.0kg/m²; 9 months ago 20.0kg/m²; 6 months ago 21.0kg/m²; 3 months ago 20.0kg/m²; current 20.8kg/m²), SBP (12 months ago 128mmHg; 9 months ago 124mmHg; 6 months ago 130mmHg; 3 months ago 130mmHg; current 124mmHg), DBP (12 months ago 87mmHg; 9 months ago 87mmHg; 6 months ago 89mmHg; 3 months ago 90mmHg; current 88mmHg)
- Liver function: ALT (12 months ago 30U/L; 9 months ago 29U/L; 6 months ago 29U/L; 3 months ago 30U/L; current 24U/L), AST (12 months ago 21U/L; 9 months ago 21U/L; 6 months ago 22U/L; 3 months ago 22U/L; current 23U/L), Total Bilirubin (12 months ago 0.4mg/dL; 9 months ago 0.5mg/dL; 6 months ago 0.5mg/dL; 3 months ago 0.5mg/dL; current 0.6mg/dL)
- Kidney: eGFR (12 months ago 16mL/min/1.73m²; 9 months ago 16mL/min/1.73m²; 6 months ago 14mL/min/1.73m²; 3 months ago 12mL/min/1.73m²; current 13mL/min/1.73m²), UACR (12 months ago 505mg/g; 9 months ago 549mg/g; 6 months ago 428mg/g; 3 months ago 622mg/g; current 680mg/g)
- Lipids: LDL (12 months ago 81mg/dL; 9 months ago 87mg/dL; 6 months ago 87mg/dL; 3 months ago 85mg/dL; current 83mg/dL); HDL (12 months ago 61mg/dL; 9 months ago 60mg/dL; 6 months ago 61mg/dL; 3 months ago 58mg/dL; current 59mg/dL); Triglycerides (12 months ago 137mg/dL; 9 months ago 138mg/dL; 6 months ago 134mg/dL; 3 months ago 122mg/dL; current 128mg/dL)
- Hemoglobin: Hemoglobin (12 months ago 11.0g/dL; 9 months ago 11.0g/dL; 6 months ago 10.2g/dL; 3 months ago 10.9g/dL; current 10.1g/dL)
- Additional tests: urine ketone Negative (Current); fasting C-peptide 0.43 ng/mL (Current)

### Individualized treatment decision factors
Current adherence and self-management: Very poor adherence (estimated <50%); SMBG rarely performed; lifestyle control severely limited.
Financial context: Patient has comprehensive insurance with minimal out-of-pocket burden and reports no financial barriers to clinically indicated therapies (budget ≤ KRW 1,000,000/month).
